frameworks.name: resource-rational analysis
frameworks.one_line: algorithm choice optimizing expected-accuracy vs computational-cost trade-off

frameworks.name: bounded rationality
frameworks.one_line: classical bounded-rational accounts unified under resource-rational frame

frameworks.name: ecological rationality
frameworks.one_line: heuristics-as-adaptations view subsumed by resource-rational principle

frameworks.name: computational-level analysis
frameworks.one_line: Marr-style optimality reinterpreted with explicit compute cost

core_question: Can human cognition be understood as the optimal use of limited computational resources?
key_assumptions: the mind selects algorithms, not just behaviors
computational cost is a constraint comparable to data limits
manipulations: review-level comparison across bounded / ecological / computational frames
application of resource-rational frame to heuristics, attention, decision under uncertainty
key_findings: rationality principle operationalizes as near-optimal accuracy-cost trade-off
resource-rational frame unifies bounded-rational, ecological-rational, and computational-level accounts
frame yields predictions about heuristics, attention allocation, and decision under uncertainty
interpretations: cognitive heuristics are rational solutions to bounded compute, not failures
a single optimization principle can integrate three previously separate rationality traditions
